klipperscreenStudio.intro: Edit your printer's KlipperScreen touchscreen configuration safely, then restart KlipperScreen to apply. Every save is backed up first.

steps: Edit the configuration text below (or leave it as-is).
Save — the current file is backed up first, and the save is refused while the printer is busy.
Restart KlipperScreen to apply the change on the physical screen.
Lines use UNIX (LF) endings, enforced automatically on save.

glossary.title: Glossary
glossary.body: Quick definitions of the KlipperScreen terms used here.

config.title: What's the Config tab?
config.body: The raw KlipperScreen.conf. Edit any setting by hand; the save backs the file up first, is refused while the printer is busy, and warns if the file changed on the screen since you loaded it. Most people use the Settings / Menus / Themes tabs instead.

settings.title: What's the Settings tab?
settings.body: The most-used [main] options as a form — theme, language, screen sleep, font size, and toggles. Pre-filled from the live config; only the options you change are written. Restart to apply.

menus.title: What's the Menus tab?
menus.body: Design the touchscreen's button grids. Each tree (__main is the home grid, __print the print screen) lists its buttons; add, rename, reorder and nest them, and set each to open a sub-menu, open a built-in screen, or run G-code.

themes.title: What's the Themes tab?
themes.body: Build a KlipperScreen theme: pick a colour per palette token and a corner radius, watch the live preview, then create and apply it to the screen. The preview is approximate — the real screen renders in GTK.

kiosk.title: What's the Kiosk tab?
kiosk.body: Put FilaMind Flow itself on the touchscreen as a fullscreen browser — total control of what the screen shows. It's a reversible swap with KlipperScreen (temporary by default; a reboot restores KlipperScreen). Needs the one-time installer on the printer host.

klipperScreen.def: The touchscreen UI that runs on a small display attached to the printer — separate from this web panel.

theme.def: A KlipperScreen look — colours, icons and corner radius. Lives in a styles/<name> folder on the host; cosmetic only (it can't change the layout).

menu.def: The grids of buttons KlipperScreen shows. __main is the home screen, __print the screen shown while printing, __splashscreen the startup screen.

panel.def: A built-in KlipperScreen screen a button can open (move, temperature, extrude, bed mesh, …). Their internal contents are fixed by KlipperScreen.

autoBlock.def: The section KlipperScreen writes for itself at the bottom of the conf, after the #~# marker. FilaMind never edits it — only your settings above it.

kiosk.def: Running a fullscreen browser on the touchscreen instead of KlipperScreen, so the screen shows FilaMind Flow. Fully reversible.

status.absent: KlipperScreen was not detected on this printer (no KlipperScreen.conf, and not reported by Moonraker). Install KlipperScreen to use this tool.
status.notRestartable: Moonraker can't restart KlipperScreen automatically — restart it manually after saving.

save.busy: Refused while the printer is busy — try again when it's idle.
save.stale: KlipperScreen.conf changed on disk since it was loaded (likely edited on the screen). Reload before saving so you don't overwrite that change.

settings.intro: The most-used KlipperScreen display options as a form. Only options you change are written — everything else keeps its current value. Restart to apply.
settings.onlyChanged: Only the settings you change here are written to KlipperScreen.conf.

label.use_dpms: Use DPMS to blank the screen

kiosk.intro: Put FilaMind Flow itself on the touchscreen as a fullscreen browser — total control of what the screen shows. It swaps with KlipperScreen: starting one stops the other.
kiosk.setupHint: Run this once on the printer host (installs a fullscreen browser + the kiosk service):
kiosk.persist: Make it the default at boot (otherwise the swap is temporary — a reboot restores the other).
kiosk.safety: Temporary by default: a reboot brings the other one back. If the screen ever goes dark, over SSH run: sudo systemctl start KlipperScreen
kiosk.confirmBody: This stops one screen service and starts the other. It doesn't affect an active print, but the touchscreen will go black for a few seconds while it switches.
kiosk.switchedNote: FilaMind is taking over the screen — it should appear in a few seconds.
kiosk.restoredNote: KlipperScreen is coming back — the screen should return shortly.

menus.intro: Design what the touchscreen shows: each menu's buttons, their order, and what each does. The home-screen grid is the __main tree; the print screen is __print.
menus.name: Name
menus.savedRestart: Menus saved — restart KlipperScreen to see them.

themes.previewApprox: Approximate preview — the real screen renders with GTK; minor differences are expected.
themes.created: Theme created. Apply it from the list, then restart.
themes.appliedRestart: Theme set — restart KlipperScreen to see it.

restart.done: KlipperScreen is restarting — the screen will reload shortly.
restart.confirmBody: This restarts the touchscreen service to apply your changes. It won't affect an active print, but the screen goes blank for a few seconds.
